title: Warmly Spiced Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A savory and indulgent Egyptian dish featuring pomegranate molasses, this warmly spiced lamb tagine is cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot, resulting in a rich and glassy jelly-like sauce. It is an ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly recipe that blends traditional Egyptian flavors with a unique cooking method.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot by placing it in a cold oven and setting the temperature to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the pomegranate molasses, olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Remove the tandoor clay pot from the oven and carefully add the lamb and marinade to the pot.
Cover the pot with a lid and return it to the oven, baking for 2 1/2 hours.
After 2 hours, remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened into a glassy jelly-like consistency.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the warmly spiced Egyptian lamb tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
